_readme: VERDICT 058 fixtures — every registered constant, copied verbatim from the PROPOSAL 047 block (idea-engine control/outbox.md @ main 2808b16, landed via idea-engine PR #350) and the idea doc ideas/superbot/creature-rarity-skill-counter-2026-07-13.md @ the same pin; engine constants and all 24 quoted catalog rows (18 used: 6 Epics + 12 Commons; plus the 6 Rares for the gradient leg) verified verbatim against menno420/superbot @ 1cc553651a19016a4b1439f048b49e7baa28dfb1 (disbot/utils/creatures/battle.py, creature.py, encounters.py, disbot/data/creatures/creatures.json). Committed BEFORE the runner (git-trail discipline). The runner reads ONLY this file.

harvested_lines_verbatim.rarity_budget_comment: Total stat budget per rarity — rarer = stronger, but level + type + move choice still let a Common counter an Epic.
harvested_lines_verbatim.module_docstring_line: Flat-level PvP makes **types + team-building + ordering + move choice** decide, so it rewards *skill*, not *time spent*.

effectiveness_rule: attack element strong (1.5) vs the next two on the cycle (delta=(d-a)%6 in {1,2}), weak (0.67) vs the previous two (delta in {4,5}), neutral (1.0) vs itself and its opposite; Normal always 1.0

effectiveness_hand_table_F1._order: rows = attack type (6 elements + Normal), columns = defender element in element_cycle order

stat_derivation: round(budget * weight / total) per stat (hp, atk, def, spd), total = hp_w + atk_w + df_w + spd_w, Python-3 banker's rounding on the float expression exactly as battle.py derive_stats computes it

derived_stats_gate_F2._note: the six used (rarity, archetype) pairs, hand-pinned incl. the committed tank-Epic 301 overshoot

gate_F3.atk_df_level_invariance: 60/50 = 1.2 at any level (the off mult cancels in every atk/df ratio); asserted numerically at levels 5 and 50 within 1e-12 of 6/5

moves.set: Strike (Normal, power 9) / element signature (power 12, type chart applies) / Bulwark (+DEF) / Onslaught (+ATK)

damage_formula: max(1, round((atk / max(1.0, df)) * power * mult * jitter)), jitter ~ rng.uniform(0.85, 1.0); expected_damage uses the constant 0.925 in place of jitter

battle_rules.structure: 6v6 lead-until-faint; each turn both leads act in SPD order
battle_rules.tie: exact SPD tie resolved by one rng.random() < 0.5 coin flip per turn (a-first if < 0.5)
battle_rules.ko_denial: a KO denies the victim's action that turn (engine: `if actor.fainted or target.fainted: continue`)
battle_rules.stall_guard_semantics: any battle reaching it invalidates the run (unmodeled territory); the engine awards the higher remaining-HP side
battle_rules.winner_rule: loop exits when either index reaches team length; winner 'a' iff the b index is exhausted (engine: 'a' if ib >= len(team_b) else 'b')

catalog_rows_verbatim._note: quoted verbatim @ superbot 1cc5536 disbot/data/creatures/creatures.json — [element, rarity, archetype]

catalog_shape_guard: 36-row catalog @ 1cc5536: exactly one Epic per element, two Commons per element (one balanced, one attacker); a catalog reshuffle or re-tuned engine means re-run, not reuse

teams._note: all pre-orders pinned to ELEMENT_CYCLE order; MIX = balanced on even ELEMENT_CYCLE indices (Ember, Bramble, Stone), attacker on odd (Tide, Spark, Gust)

pilots.common_side_always: MAX-SKILL — order_type_aware (stable-sort the team by descending effectiveness vs the opponent's PRE-ordering lead) + policy_setup (best expected-damage move; finish a kill rather than buff; one opening +ATK when un-buffed, cur_hp >= 0.6*max_hp, and not slower)
pilots.BEGINNER: policy_naive_element (always the signature move, even into a 0.67 resist) with the pinned ELEMENT_CYCLE lead order
pilots.MID: policy_best_damage (higher expected damage of Strike vs signature each turn), pinned lead order
pilots.ordering_resolution: both type-aware orderings resolve against the opponent's PRE-ordering lead, applied simultaneously (the engine gives ordering no second-mover privilege)

grid.metric: W(cell) = P(Common side wins) as an exact fractions.Fraction of wins/N

seeds.registry_note: 20261325–328 strictly above the P046/V057 high-water 20261324; aux is registered-never-drawn — no RNG may be constructed with it (asserted)

main_leg_order: one random.Random(20261325) stream: the 9-cell grid (compositions outer BAL/ATK/MIX, pilots inner BEGINNER/MID/SKILLED), then reporting legs in pinned order: mirror (BAL vs BAL, both MAX-SKILL), naive-vs-naive (BAL vs Epic, both policy_naive_element, pinned pre-orders), Rare-gradient BEGINNER (BAL MAX-SKILL vs Rare team naive), Rare-gradient SKILLED (BAL MAX-SKILL vs Rare team setup+ordering)
stability_leg: random.Random(20261326), the 9-cell grid at N = 5000, pinned order; must reproduce the ruling class through both twin evaluators

jitter_degenerate._note: fixture-only hook: damage jitter pinned to the expected-damage constant, no uniform draw (sentinel pinned 0); tie flips still drawn

buff_cap_75._note: derived disclosure: the committed policy family buffs at most once (policy_setup requires atk_stage == 0.0), so buff stages never exceed 0.25 and this lever is inert by construction — run and reported anyway per the registration

sensitivity_note: all four scenarios ride one random.Random(20261327) stream in the pinned order, 9-cell grid at N = 5000 each, reporting-only — they name axes, never flip the decision

decision_bands.reject_rule: W(c, BEGINNER) < 2/5 in >= 2 of 3 Common compositions — checked FIRST
decision_bands.approve_rule: W(c, BEGINNER) >= 1/2 in ALL 3 compositions AND W(c, SKILLED) < 1/2 in >= 2 of 3 AND the seed-20261326 stability leg reproduces the ruling through both twin evaluators
null_axes: sensitivity straddle (reporting legs name the axis, never flip the decision)
decision_bands.evaluation_order: REJECT first, then APPROVE, else NULL
decision_bands.se_precheck: SE at N = 20000 is <= 1/283; the decision bands (2/5 vs 1/2) sit >= 14 SE apart

arm_e.per_encounter_p: P(catch specific creature c) = (weight_c / 1884) * catch_c at player level 50
arm_e.expected_encounters: E[max over the 6 team targets] by max-min inclusion-exclusion: E = sum over nonempty S of (-1)^(|S|+1) / sum_{c in S} p_c (exact under per-encounter exclusivity — one creature per encounter)
arm_e.markov_twin: subset-state Markov chain E(S) = 1/P_S + sum_{c in S} (p_c / P_S) * E(S minus c), P_S = sum of p_c over S — must agree with inclusion-exclusion at exact equality
arm_e.outputs: E_epic (the committed all-Epic team), E_common (BAL), TP = E_epic / E_common (reporting-only for the decision; the verdict's stakes line)
arm_e.tp_hand_span: approx 39.6 (the exact Fraction is the sim's output; the two hand anchors are the gates)

hand_fixture_F4._note: deterministic 1v1 hand trace: Cindling (Common balanced, level 50) vs Infernox (Epic tank, level 50), both policy_best_damage, jitter pinned = 0.925 (fixture-only hook, zero jitter draws). Cindling acts first every turn (spd 135.75 > 122.175); both pick the signature move (Cinderlash, Ember vs Ember = neutral 1.0, expected 12*0.925 > 9*0.925); per-action damages exactly 6 = round(12 * 0.925 * 135.75/266.07) and 13 = round(12 * 0.925 * 1.2); Infernox KOs on its 16th action with Cindling having dealt 96 = 16*6 <= 386; winner b. Full 32-event log below: [turn, side, actor, move, damage, target_hp_left, faint], turns 1..16, per turn Cindling then Infernox; Infernox hp after Cindling's t-th action = 386 - 6t; Cindling hp after Infernox's t-th action = max(0, 197 - 13t).

gates.F1: the full 7x6 effectiveness hand table exact (incl. Normal row = 1.0, opposite-element = 1.0)
gates.F2: derived-stats hand table for all six used (rarity, archetype) pairs, banker's-rounding values pinned, incl. the tank-Epic 301 overshoot
gates.F3: level-50 identities: HP mult 3.94 and off mult 2.715 exact; balanced Common max_hp 197; tank Epic max_hp 386; atk/df ratio level-invariance (60/50 = 1.2 at levels 5 and 50, within 1e-12 of 6/5 — the off mult cancels symbolically)
gates.F4: the deterministic hand-traced Cindling-vs-Infernox 1v1 fixture at jitter = 0.925: full event log exact, damages 6 and 13, Cindling first, Infernox KOs on its 16th action, Cindling dealt 96
gates.F5: mirror match within 1/50 of 1/2 (invalidating); naive-vs-naive W < 1/2 expected-direction (loudly flagged, not invalidating); zero stall-guard hits (invalidating); RARITY_BUDGET spread 300/200 = 3/2 asserted from the pinned table
gates.F6: Arm E anchors: 785 exact; the 49/20 equal-p identity exact; the subset-Markov twin agreeing with inclusion-exclusion at exact equality
gates.sentinels: per-leg draw-count sentinels: jitter draws = damage events (except the pinned-0 degenerate scenario), tie-flip draws = recorded ties
gates.twins: twin independently-written decision evaluators (Fraction comparisons vs pure-integer cross-multiplication, opposite iteration orders) agree on main AND stability inputs
gates.reproducibility: stdout + results.json byte-identical across two process runs (external diff); Arm E platform-independent exact Fractions; the battle arm pinned to CPython 3.11 (asserted)

boundaries.skill_ceiling: the policy family is the engine's own committed skill ceiling (four policies + one ordering lever; no mid-battle switching exists in the engine) — a cleverer in-engine policy could only RAISE the Common side, so REJECT is conservative in the direction that matters
boundaries.float: the battle arm's reproducibility is pinned to a stated CPython minor (the P017+ Arm-S convention)
boundaries.roster: 6v6 element-complete teams only (the owner's standard); off-meta element stacking out of registered scope
boundaries.level: the harvested comment's 'level' lever is normalized away in ranked PvP by the engine's own NORMALIZED_LEVEL rule — this prices the sharper surviving claim; raw-level PvE counters out of scope
boundaries.trading: trading/gifting economies don't exist in the engine; TP prices self-caught grind only
